title: refactor(enter): remove dead bindings, config, and unreachable helpers
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Removes unread wrangler.toml bindings from all 6 enter envs: IMAGE_BUCKET, TEXT_BUCKET, EDGE_RATE_LIMITER, POLLEN_REFILL_PER_HOUR, ALLOW_ANONYMOUS_USAGE (78 lines; only consumers are in gen, which declares its own copies); regenerates worker-bindings.d.ts
- Removes isLoopbackUrl + its test — zero callers; the comment claiming the consent UI uses it was stale. redirectUriMatchesAllowlist re-export kept (used by app-lookup.ts)
- Removes pollenTierMeterId/pollenPackMeterId from frontend config — zero consumers since b888fde64
- Drops the last SSR guard in sign-in.tsx (straggler from #11473; client-only SPA)
- Removes unused deps: nanoid, neverthrow, hono-rate-limiter, @hono-rate-limiter/cloudflare (last usage removed in the Nov 2025 token-bucket commit)

Not included: sendTierEventToTinybird logger cleanup (src/events.ts owned by a sibling infra-modules PR) and gen wrangler.toml's ALLOW_ANONYMOUS_USAGE (owned by the gen cleanup PR).

Verified: tsc --noEmit clean, url-utils tests 13/13, integration/api-keys 31/31 against trimmed wrangler.toml, frontend vite build OK.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: Sweeping up the hive
gist.blurb: We did some spring cleaning in the auth gateway, sweeping away unused code, old bindings, and forgotten dependencies. A lighter codebase is a happier codebase!
gist.summary: Removed 78 lines of unused wrangler.toml bindings, dead helper functions like isLoopbackUrl, and stale dependencies from the enter.pollinations.ai gateway.
gist.impact: This internal cleanup reduces deployment size and simplifies the auth gateway codebase for future development.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee doing some spring cleaning, sweeping up dead leaves/code snippets, while the Nomnom creature happily eats the discarded code.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny bandana, sweeping up small pixelated dead leaves and old code snippets with a tiny broom. The round tan nomnom creature happily eats a stack of discarded documents. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and warm cream gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
